content.full_text:
Farn Polrrrcar- PRACTrcps C oMMrssroN
P.O. Box 807 o 428 J Street . Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
November 7,2001
Larry McConnell
Executive Director
Sonoma County Alliance
637 Ftst Street
Santa Rosa, CA 95404
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. ,4.-01-245
Dear Mr. McConnell
This is in response to your letter requesting advice on behalf of the Sonoma
County Alliance Political Action Committee regarding the campaign provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1
QUESTIONS
May the Sonoma County Alliance Political Action Committee (PAC) reimburse
its sponsoring organization for expenditures made on its behalf? Are there any
restrictions to this reimbursement policy?
CONCLUSIONS
The PAC may reimburse its sponsor for certain expenses. Please review the
Analysis section of this letter for restrictions on reimbursements.
FACTS
The Sonoma County Alliance Political Action Committee is a recipient committee
sponsored by the Sonoma County Alliance. The PAC supports only candidates for local
office in Sonoma County. It is the intention of the sponsor to provide use of offrce space
' 
Goverrunent Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulalions appear attttle 2, sections
18109 - 78997, of the California Code of Regulations.
@B


November 7,2001
Page Two
and equipment, postage, and photocopying services to the PAC, in addition to paying for
travel-related expenses for the PAC. In return, the PAC intends to reimburse its sponsor
for these expenses.
ANALYSIS
Section 89512.5 controls the use of funds by a committee not controlled by a
candidate. Generally, any committee expenditure reasonably related to a political,
legislative, or governmental purpose is permitted. If an expenditure confers a substantial
personal benefit on an individual who has authority to approve an expenditure of
campaign funds held by the committee, then such an expenditure must be directly related
to the political, legislative, or governmental purpose of the committee.
Section 89513(bXl) specifically permits the use of committee funds for
professional services when they are "reasonably required by the committee to assist it in
the performance of its administrative functions" and those functions are directly related to
a p olitic al, legi sl ative, or governmental purp o se.
Therefore, as long as no individual who has authority to approve an expenditure
of the PAC's funds receives a substantial personal benefit from the reimbursements made
by the PAC to its sponsor, and the expenses are reasonably related to a political,
legislative, or governmental purpose, the reimbursements are permitted. This could
include payments for office space, office equipment, postage, photocopies, and travel.
Enclosed are Government Code sections 89512 5 through 89517 which concern
the use of campaign funds by recipient committees. In addition, two advice letters (Rice-
Laabs No. 4-96-110 and Porisi No. I-98-057) are enclosed to further assist you in
understanding what types of expenditures are permitted.
If you have any further questions, please do not hesitate to contact me.
Sincerely,
Luisa
PhD
Enclosures
Bv
cal Reform Consultant II
